steps.title: Read the radius value
steps.content:
A circle in standard form has equation $(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2$. In the given equation, the right side is the radius squared.
$$x^2+(y-5)^2=7$$

steps.title: Use the unchanged radius
steps.content: A translation changes the center but does not change the radius. Therefore, circle B still has $a=7$ in the equation $(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=a$.
